bead.title: routing P0: repo isolation + dirty-repo guard with cleanup/revert contract
bead.description: codex/claude blocking: failed and (later) local attempts must run in isolated git worktrees with a defined cleanup/revert contract; the worker must NOT drain a dirty/WIP repo (helix churned on this). PROBLEM: lifecycle dispatch rejects dirty roots (correct) but there is no clean isolation/cleanup contract for failed-then-escalated attempts, and dirty WIP repos churn the queue. PROPOSED: ensure every attempt (incl. escalation retries) runs in an isolated worktree; on failure, revert/clean the worktree before the next attempt; never operate on a dirty canonical checkout; define artifact retention. This must land BEFORE escalation + local mixin. cross-ref FEAT-004 addendum.
bead.acceptance: 1. Failed attempt leaves the canonical repo clean; escalation retry starts from a clean isolated worktree. 2. Worker refuses to drain a dirty/WIP root with a clear operator-attention signal (no churn loop). 3. Test reproduces the helix-style dirty-WIP churn and asserts it no longer loops. 4. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green. 5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
